text.title: Package Patterns: Config, Model, Repository, Service
text.summary: Constructor injection, interface-at-consumer pattern, typed config, and DI wiring for the internal/ layer.

rule.statement: Use a typed config loader (envconfig, koanf, viper) rather than hand-rolled getEnv helpers. Hand-rolled helpers silently return defaults on type-parse failures.
rule.rationale: A getEnv that returns "" on missing PORT instead of an error lets the server start on the wrong port silently. Typed loaders fail loudly at startup.

rule.statement: Define interfaces at the consumer (the service), not the producer (the repository). The service file owns UserRepository interface; the repository package provides the concrete impl.
rule.rationale: This is idiomatic Go: the interface lives with the code that uses it, not the code that satisfies it. It avoids forcing all consumers to import the repository package just to get an interface.

antipattern.description: Defining the interface in the repository package and importing it in the service.
antipattern.reason: Inverts the dependency — service must import repository. Idiomatic Go reverses this.

antipattern.description: var DB *sql.DB at package level, accessed directly from handlers.
antipattern.reason: Global state is untestable (cannot inject a mock), causes data races when reset between tests, and makes the dependency graph invisible.
